role_title: Strategic Partnerships & Growth Lead — DoW / US Army

level_signals: 5+ years of channel sales, BD, or partnership management experience
This is a senior individual contributor role with revenue ownership, reporting directly to sales leadership
Owns a revenue target for Army/DoD prime channel business

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Active Secret clearance or above — candidates without an active clearance will not be considered
5+ years of channel sales, BD, or partnership management experience inside the defense industrial base, with a focus on Army-aligned programs
Existing relationships at one or more major Army prime contractors (SAIC, Leidos, Booz Allen, CACI, Peraton) at the BD, program management, or technical leadership level
Understanding of how prime contractors evaluate, approve, and deploy software tools across their development teams and program deliveries
Track record of standing up or expanding channel and partner relationships that drove material software revenue ($500K+)
Comfortable working both the prime-facing channel layer (BD, program managers, contracts) and the end-user pull-through layer (Army software developers, Software Factory operators)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Prior active duty or civilian service in an Army software or digital modernization role (Software Factory, PEO, Army Futures Command)
Experience selling developer tools, AI/ML platforms, or SDLC tooling through or alongside defense primes
Familiarity with how Army programs evaluate and mandate developer tooling standards across prime and subcontractor teams
Existing Army-side relationships at Kessel Run, PEO EIS, Army CIO/G-6, or Futures Command

explicitly_excluded: Candidates without an active clearance will not be considered

scope.surface: Army/DoD prime contractor channel — building partnerships with defense primes (SAIC, Leidos, Booz Allen Hamilton, CACI, Peraton) and generating Army end-user pull-through
scope.business_model: enterprise B2B / channel sales within defense industrial base
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue target for Army/DoD prime channel business
Pipeline of prime contractor partnerships and program-level co-sell opportunities
Enterprise agreement volume with defense primes
Developer seat expansion across defense industrial base
core_responsibilities: Own and grow Cursor's relationships with defense prime contractors focused on Army programs
Negotiate and close partnership agreements — reseller arrangements, teaming agreements, and approved vendor designations
Work with prime contractor BD and program management teams to co-sell Cursor into new Army contract awards at the proposal stage
Build relationships inside Army Software Factories (Kessel Run, Project Origen, Castle Rock) and program offices
Engage Army CIO/G-6, PEO EIS, and Futures Command tech organizations
Represent Cursor at AUSA, NDIA, and Army-focused industry events
Own a revenue target for Army/DoD prime channel business
Build and manage a pipeline of prime contractor partnerships and program-level co-sell opportunities
Forecast and report on channel pipeline with the same rigor as direct enterprise deals
Partner with Field Engineering on technical evaluations and POCs
Work with Cursor's legal and compliance teams on teaming agreements and FedRAMP/IL considerations
Channel Army developer feedback and product gaps back to Cursor's roadmap team

green_flags: Clear ownership: senior IC with direct revenue accountability and reporting line to sales leadership
Well-defined target account list (SAIC, Leidos, Booz Allen, CACI, Peraton) — not a vague market
Cursor already has existing prime contractor presence — role builds on warm foundation rather than cold start
Direct line to GTM and product leadership mentioned explicitly
Specific Army ecosystem touchpoints named (Kessel Run, PEO EIS, CIO/G-6, Futures Command) — JD shows genuine domain knowledge
red_flags: Location/remote policy not stated at all — unclear where candidate must be based
Active Secret clearance hard requirement eliminates most candidates and narrows pool severely
No visa sponsorship is possible given security clearance requirements — not stated but practically implied
Revenue target amount not disclosed, making it difficult to assess quota pressure
JD does not clarify whether this is a new function being built from scratch with no team support

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type set to 'unclear' — no office location, remote policy, or city mentioned anywhere in JD
location.work_auth set to 'us-citizenship-required' — Secret clearance and above requires US citizenship; not explicitly stated but practically required
location.visa_sponsorship set to 'no' — inferred from security clearance requirement
seniority.level inferred from 'senior individual contributor' label and 5+ years requirement
scope.business_model inferred from product description and channel motion described
scope.primary_metrics_inferred — specific numbers not given, inferred from responsibilities
compensation.equity_mentioned false — equity not mentioned in JD
ambiguities: Archetype is ambiguous: the role is labeled 'Strategic Partnerships & Growth Lead' and involves revenue ownership, but it is not a classic PM or program manager role; mapped to BizOps/Strategy & Ops as closest fit with secondary note of Channel Sales/Partnerships
Work location is entirely unstated — could be remote, hybrid, or DC/Northern Virginia-area onsite, which is common for defense-focused roles
Security clearance work auth: JD does not explicitly state US citizenship is required, but Active Secret clearance effectively mandates it
